Package Index | Class Index
Summary: Constants | Fields | Ctors | Methods | Protected Methods | Inherited Methods | [Expand All]
public class

FastDataInput

extends Object
implements DataInput Closeable
java.lang.Object
   ↳ com.android.modules.utils.FastDataInput

Class Overview

Optimized implementation of DataInput which buffers data in memory from the underlying InputStream.

Benchmarks have demonstrated this class is 3x more efficient than using a DataInputStream with a BufferedInputStream.

Summary

int MAX_UNSIGNED_SHORT

public void close ()

Throws
IOException

public byte[] newByteArray (int bufferSize)

Parameters
bufferSize int
Returns
byte[]

public static FastDataInput obtain (InputStream in)

Obtain a FastDataInput configured with the given InputStream and which encodes large code-points using 3-byte sequences.

This is compatible with the DataInput API contract, which specifies that large code-points must be encoded with 3-byte sequences.

Parameters
in InputStream
Returns
FastDataInput

public byte peekByte ()

Returns the same decoded value as readByte() but without actually consuming the underlying data.

Returns
byte
Throws
IOException

public String readInternedUTF ()

Read a String value with the additional signal that the given value is a candidate for being canonicalized, similar to String.intern().

Canonicalization is implemented by writing each unique string value once the first time it appears, and then writing a lightweight short reference when that string is written again in the future.

Returns
String
Throws
IOException

See also:

  • FastDataOutput.writeInternedUTF(String)
